seoTitle: How to Import and Export Data in Airtable Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to import CSV and spreadsheet files into Airtable and export your records back out for backup, reporting, or use in Excel.
intro: Whether you're bringing existing spreadsheet data into Airtable or pulling records out for reporting and backup, the import and export tools cover both directions. This guide walks through uploading CSV and spreadsheet files, exporting tables back out, and fixing the common issues that come up along the way.

steps.heading: Import data into Airtable
steps.body: Start the import process from your base to bring in existing data rather than manually recreating records.

steps.heading: Upload CSV and spreadsheet files
steps.body: Select your CSV or spreadsheet file and map its columns to Airtable fields during the upload process.

steps.heading: Export Airtable data
steps.body: Use the export option on a table or view to pull your records back out in a portable format.

steps.heading: Download and use exported files
steps.body: Save the exported file locally so you can open it in Excel, Google Sheets, or another tool for backup or analysis.

steps.heading: Watch for common import issues
steps.body: Check for column mismatches or formatting problems that can cause data to land in the wrong fields during import.

faq.question: What file formats can I import into Airtable?
faq.answer: CSV and spreadsheet files are the standard formats supported for bringing outside data into an Airtable base.

faq.question: Can I export my Airtable table to Excel?
faq.answer: Yes, exported CSV files can be opened directly in Excel or Google Sheets for further analysis.

faq.question: Why did my import put data in the wrong fields?
faq.answer: This usually happens when column headers in your source file don't match Airtable's field names — checking the mapping step during import helps prevent it.

faq.question: Is exporting data useful for backups?
faq.answer: Yes, periodically exporting your tables is a simple way to keep an offline backup of your Airtable data.
